title: IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE - 211info
text: PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a Determination of Homelessness form needed to apply for an ID Card from the Oregon Driver & Motor Vehicle Services (DMV). The agency does not provide driver’s licenses or birth certificates. 1440 SE 21st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-5pm Email (971) 713-3930 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (971) 713-3930 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for more information FEES: None; including cost of ID Card from the DMV DOCUMENTS: Backup identification documentation is required. For example, the DMV typically requires a birth certificate before issuing an ID card. For information about DMV requirements, ask a staff person or call the local DMV. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Washington County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 57 stops two blocks away. Use entrance at the east end. Services: Records/Licenses/Permits Fee Payment Assistance * Homelessness Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - ACCESS CENTER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT
